title: Melicope macrocarpa
intro: Melicope macrocarpa, also known as Hawaiian allspice or Neiu, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to Hawaii. The plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its health benefits, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Melicope macrocarpa include flavonoids, terpenoids, and coumarins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Hawaiian herbal medicine, Melicope macrocarpa is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to promote relaxation, reduce fever, and support overall well-being.
